assumptions: The intragroup dividend receivable/payable is a monetary item denominated in a currency that creates foreign exchange exposure between group entities with different functional currencies.
The related foreign exchange gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation and can affect consolidated profit or loss.
The general IFRS 9 hedge accounting designation, documentation and effectiveness requirements can be met.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognized intragroup dividend receivable is a recognized monetary asset whose foreign currency risk may be designated in consolidation if the resulting FX gains or losses are not fully eliminated.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The foreign currency component of the settlement cash flows on a recognized intragroup receivable can also be considered under the cash flow hedge model if that is the risk being managed and the intragroup exception is met.
